li.text:
Warning klaxons howl through the cabin as your airship tears itself apart over the wasteland. Three of you barely make it clear before the hull breaks open.
When the smoke clears, you are alive, armed, and stranded beside the burning wreck of a pre-war aircraft. If you cannot raise shelter from twisted metal and scrap, the wasteland will finish what the crash started.

value.text:
The old stories were right. The metal devils came in the night with fire, blades, and screaming engines. Your tribe fell before dawn.
Five of you survived the slaughter. Hungry, grieving, and alone, you now stand in a harsh land that offers nothing freely. Build again, and make sure your people do not vanish here.

Life in the vault was safe, sealed, and dull. You grew up with polished corridors, filtered air, and the promise that the world outside was only death.
You left anyway. Now the hatch opens onto a raw stretch of wasteland, and all you have are fine weapons, vault provisions, and your own nerve. Out here, no door closes behind you.

Your mouth tastes like blood and dust. Whoever beat you, robbed you, and left you naked out here is long gone.
The sun is rising, the ground is cold, and every living thing around you would happily finish the job. Stand up, find something useful, and prove the wasteland picked the wrong corpse.
